{"society_power": 6.0, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Polarization, Jan 6, and executive-power concentration", "reasoning": "Intense partisan polarization, declining institutional trust, and fragmented media eroded society's cohesive capacity to check the state even as protest surges (Floyd, anti-mandate) showed persistent mobilization, netting a modest decline. State power holds high: COVID-era fiscal/regulatory expansion and surveillance capacity offset norm-damage and administrative-deference rollbacks, leaving net state capacity roughly stable."}
